Rule: Verify customer identity using an independent, trusted source before account creation or start of the relationship
Scenario: A customer attempts to register without completing verification via Nafath or Yaqeen
Expected Behavior: Reject account creation until identity is successfully verified

Rule: All core customer data must be collected and verified before opening the account
Scenario: A customer submits incomplete or partial personal data
Expected Behavior: Prevent account activation until all required data is completed and verified

Rule: The system must accurately support reading Arabic-language documents
Scenario: Customer uploads an Arabic ID or residency permit and the system fails to read the data
Expected Behavior: Halt activation and route the case to manual review

Rule: Verify investor eligibility and the authorized agent before allowing investment
Scenario: A minor or an agent attempts to invest without regulatory authorization
Expected Behavior: Reject the transaction until regulatory requirements are met

Rule: Continuously monitor the validity of identity documents
Scenario: An investor attempts to execute a transaction after their ID has expired
Expected Behavior: Suspend the transaction until the identity document is updated

Rule: Detect funds or assets originating from an illegitimate source
Scenario: A customer deposits a large amount from an unclear source to purchase tokenized assets
Expected Behavior: Raise the risk level and suspend the transaction pending verification of the source of funds

Rule: Monitor financial transactions in real time
Scenario: Execution of a series of unusual financial transactions within a short period
Expected Behavior: Issue an immediate alert and escalate the transaction for review

Rule: Retain transaction and compliance records for the statutory retention period
Scenario: A regulator requests review of an old transaction
Expected Behavior: Provide all required records without any data loss

Rule: Report suspicious transactions to the relevant authority
Scenario: A transaction is detected that is suspected to involve money laundering
Expected Behavior: Draft a suspicious activity report and escalate it to the compliance officer

Rule: The customer must not be notified that they are under suspicion
Scenario: The customer inquires about the reason their transaction was suspended
Expected Behavior: Do not disclose any information indicating an investigation or report exists

Rule: Identify the beneficial owner when the statutory ownership threshold is exceeded
Scenario: A company invests through a multi-layered ownership structure
Expected Behavior: Identify the beneficial owner and verify their identity

Rule: Identify the person with effective control when there is no clear direct owner
Scenario: No shareholder exceeds the defined ownership percentage
Expected Behavior: Treat the person with effective control, or senior management, as the beneficial owner

Rule: Verify beneficial owner data against official records
Scenario: Company data differs from data in the "Wathq" registry
Expected Behavior: Suspend the account until the data is corrected

Rule: Update beneficial owner data within the statutory deadline
Scenario: Ownership structure changes without updating the data
Expected Behavior: Raise an alert and suspend some services until the update is completed

Rule: Detect attempts to conceal the beneficial owner
Scenario: A sham ownership structure exists to hide the true owner
Expected Behavior: Classify the case as high risk and apply Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) procedures

Rule: Monitor all transactions in real time
Scenario: Repeated transactions executed within a short time window
Expected Behavior: Issue an alert and suspend the transaction for review

Rule: Compare transactions against the customer's expected financial behavior
Scenario: An investment is executed that is inconsistent with the customer's income
Expected Behavior: Raise the risk level and request additional information

Rule: Detect accounts used for rapidly passing funds through
Scenario: An account receives several transfers and immediately forwards them to a tokenized asset
Expected Behavior: Freeze the transaction and issue a suspicion alert

Rule: Detect attempts to split transactions to bypass regulatory thresholds
Scenario: A large amount is split into several smaller transactions
Expected Behavior: Treat the transactions as a single transaction and halt it

Rule: Maintain a tamper-proof audit log of all transactions
Scenario: Review of a previous decision to suspend a transaction
Expected Behavior: Display the complete audit trail

Rule: Screen all customers against sanctions lists before and during the relationship
Scenario: Registration of a customer listed on sanctions lists
Expected Behavior: Reject registration and freeze the account

Rule: Support approximate (fuzzy) name matching
Scenario: A slight variation in how the customer's name is written compared to the listed name
Expected Behavior: Detect the match and escalate it for review

Rule: Screen the beneficial owner against sanctions lists as well
Scenario: The beneficial owner is listed on a sanctions list even though the company itself is not
Expected Behavior: Reject the transaction and freeze the account

Rule: Reject transfers originating from sanctioned entities or countries
Scenario: A transfer arrives from a high-risk country
Expected Behavior: Reject the transfer and apply enhanced due diligence procedures

Rule: Re-screen customers whenever sanctions lists are updated
Scenario: A customer is added to a sanctions list after their account was opened
Expected Behavior: Freeze assets and halt all transactions immediately

Rule: Classify customers by risk level
Scenario: A high-risk customer registers
Expected Behavior: Apply enhanced due diligence procedures

Rule: Establish the source of funds and the purpose of the investment
Scenario: The customer does not disclose the source of funds
Expected Behavior: Suspend the account until the information is completed

Rule: Apply Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) to high-risk categories
Scenario: Registration of a Politically Exposed Person (PEP)
Expected Behavior: Require senior management approval and additional verification procedures

Rule: Any third party relied upon must be subject to regulatory oversight
Scenario: Reliance on an unlicensed verification provider
Expected Behavior: Reject the verification process and treat it as a compliance violation

Rule: Update customer data periodically
Scenario: The customer data review period has expired
Expected Behavior: Request a data update before allowing continued activity

Rule: Enforce investment limits for non-qualified investors
Scenario: An attempt to invest an amount exceeding the regulatory limit
Expected Behavior: Reject the transaction with an explanation of the reason

Rule: Verify that qualified investor requirements are met
Scenario: An upgrade request submitted with insufficient supporting documents
Expected Behavior: Reject the upgrade and escalate it for review

Rule: Enforce the maximum limit on uninvested cash
Scenario: The uninvested cash balance exceeds the permitted limit
Expected Behavior: Notify the customer and restrict certain transactions

Rule: Present regulatory disclosures before executing an investment
Scenario: An attempt to bypass the risk disclosure screen
Expected Behavior: Block the transaction until the disclosure is accepted

Rule: Segregate client funds from company funds
Scenario: The operating platform company becomes insolvent
Expected Behavior: Continue protecting investor assets and prevent commingling with company funds
